transcript.clean_text: blue skies 31 celsius a gorgeous day for the queen stage on the planet well the day after richard carapaz had made it two stage wins in the great race pretty much roglich had put pressure on ramco evernote as a result the gap overall had been narrowed one minute and 49 ever nepal's lead over roglich with mass in third place ahead of rodriguez and ayuso so how would stage 15 pan out the queen stage with some very nasty climbs at the back of it staying cool was the order of the day red hot favorite to take the stage a man who'd won here before miguel angle lopez carlos rodriguez hoping to maintain position for india's grenadiers pretty much roglich desperate to find yet more time on ramco and nepal well a quick look at the course you could see just how brutal it was alto de perche and of course the alto hoya mona hill at the end of the day never did sierra nevada look so threatening well after 35 kilometers there was a category three test that was to be raced for and would define i guess the breakaway well it had a cost particularly for wilco kelderman he fell to the side of the road got shredded did remount they had a man that would be in the eventual breakaway in jai hindley there were plenty of talented climbers up front in a breakaway of 29 well it was rack full of talent and if you wanted to get something out of the day maybe you had to go long the threat from the break was recognized by 8 g2r citroen they wanted to maintain position for ben o'connor or indeed enhance his standing in the general classification lawson credit decided he didn't like the company around him with so many climbers so he set off jay vine in the polkadot jersey also bridged over managed to get get more mountains points in the penultimate test of the day the alto de perche well as the brakes started to fracture likewise did the chase it was getting very selective out there plenty of teammates to hand for primos roglich they put two in the break quick step alpha vinyl likewise but how would those riders be used and how useful would they be well maxillaire is extraordinarily useful when he gets the chance and off he certainly looked like the day's winner meanwhile the attacks were raining down once again on remco evernote driven on by primus roglich ayuzo found himself out of position likewise almeida they weren't alone carlos rodriguez had also been caught out by the pace entering the final climb evan apoll was looking for a teammate roglic had used all of his and indeed others had the same battle plan richard cara pass dropped back to help out rodriguez and in so doing gave some assistance to ayuzo and almeida omegalanga lopez attacked the group of favourites then continued to get more selective as henrik mass went off no response from evan nepal who had louis vuitton dropped back to help him out pretty much roglich was just sitting in and seeing how this would pan out well with just under seven kilometers to go demon aaronsmen was heading for a spectacular victory the breakaway did provide our winner carlos rodriguez was struggling he would drop a place by the end of the day meanwhile ayuzo and almeida were in retrieval mode same could be said for ramco evanna paul when roglich attacked how would he respond and what would the gap be roglich pushing on with ben o'connor upon his wheel by the time we got to the line it was all going to be on countback aaronsman coming home for the biggest win of his career and you'll be hearing an awful lot more about this young dutchman going forward he crossed the line after 4 hours and 17 minutes and then we waited who would be home next rojo vine had been tagged by miguel anga lopez and mcmas crossing the line just in front of him and then roglic came home along with ben o'connor on a crucial bit of time keeping it was to be 15 seconds the gap between roglic and evanepal it looks like ever nepal has done enough to give himself a worthy cushion that may well indeed garner the title in madrid but there's a lot of racing still to come some changes of position brave finale from carlos rodriguez as well but ironsman moving into the top ten with this spectacular reach for glory on a day you'll never forget ourselves winning ahead of mass lopez j vine roglich and ben o'connor ayuso hindley menkes and then evan upon crucially 15 seconds only between roglic and ever nepal it looks like ever nepal remains in charge the margin overall cut to one minute and 34 over road glitch a ayuzo moving to fourth place rodriguez dropping to fifth miguel anger lopez in sixth spot aaronsman today's winner in eighth salvuelta heads to a rest day after which tuesday is where we resume it'll be for the sprinters don't be surprised if matt's pedestrian is up for yet more points in the green jersey competition he took the maximum on this day as well and will probably do so again la welter still six stages to go before we get to madrid you
